def: s.f. = phirkī, q.v. s.f. Whirligig, windmill (a child's toy); a reel (for thread, &c.); a spindle; anything that turns on an axis.

v.t. = phirānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be turned; to turn, to whirl, wheel; to turn round, wind, screw; to cause to go about, to move or to walk (a horse, &c.) up and down, or backwards and forwards; to turn aside or away; to return, give back; to turn back; to change; to roll, to shift
subs: v.t. To return, give back; to send back.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Turning; returning; walking about; — return; return hire or fare, expenses back; answer to a letter
subs: v.n. To keep walking about; to walk about, wander, perambulate
s.m. The return-hire (of a boat, carriage, &c.).

def: v.t. To cause to turn; to cause to walk about, &c. = phirānā, q.v.; to send round, to circulate.
intj. Pish! pshaw! tush!
v.n. To hiss
v.n. To slip, slide; to make a slip, to err
subs: v.n. To slip, &c.; — to incline (to), be attracted (towards).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Slippery, smooth.
adj. Slippery.
s.f. = phislāʼo, q.v. s.m. Slipping, sliding; slipperiness.
s.m. Slipping, sliding; slipperiness; erring, slip, error.
v.t. To cause to slip or slide; to cause to err.

ety: rt. of phislānā+āw; cf. pha sāʼo
def: s.m. Slipping, sliding; slipperiness.
v.n. To be terrified; to hiss; to spit (as a cat).
s.m. Curse, malediction; — intj. Fie! fie upon it! out upon you! a fig for it! a curse on it!
subs: intj. = phiṭ; — phiṭ-phiṭ karnā, v.t. To cry fie or shame on; to rail at, to sneer at, treat with contempt
s.m. Removing (a thing or person) to a distance, driving away; curse, &c. = phiṭ; — phiṭ-kār barasnā, v.n. Curses to be showered down (on), to be accursed
v.t. To cry fie or shame on, to turn from with contempt; to turn away with disdain; to reject; to drive away; to rail at, revile; to curse.

def: v.t. To beat up and mix; to beat up into a froth, &c. = pheṅṭnā, q.v. v.t. To mix by trituration; to beat up or whip (eggs, &c.); to beat up into froth.
v.n. To be beaten up and mixed, to be whipped (as eggs, &c.); — to be wiped off, paid off, discharged (as a debt, &c.).
intj. Faugh! pooh! pshaw! fie!
s.m. Dregs, &c. = phok, q.v. s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
adj. Four (in the language of brokers).
adj. Hollow, not solid; concave; empty; light; soft; — plain, simple, mere (also written phoṅk, phoṅkā); — phok, s.f. Hollow, concavity; notch (of an arrow).
adj. Hollow, not solid; concave; empty; light; soft; — plain, simple, mere (also written phoṅk, phoṅkā); — phok, s.f. Hollow, concavity; notch (of an arrow).

ety: phok, q.v.+Prk. लो, and लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. Hollow, &c. = phok, q.v. s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
s.m. = phok, q.v. s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
s.m. A worthless thing or person; a good-for-nothing or despicable person; an indigent person; — a thing given gratis, a gratuity
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Gratuitous, given or obtained gratis or for nothing
adv. Gratuitously, gratis, for nothing ( = saiṅt-meṅ)

def: s.f. Soft or porous soil (which readily yields to the plough).

ety: phok, q.v.+Prk. लो, and लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. Hollow, &c. = phok, q.v. s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
s.m. A blister ( = phapholā); — a skein of cotton, &c.
v.t. (local), To unfasten, untie, undo.
adj. & s.f. Hollow, &c. ( = phok).
s.f. A hollow reed, a pipe, tube; anything perforated or hollow that can be blown through).
adj. = phaphsā, q.v. adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
s.m. Boil, sore, tumour, abscess, imposthume, ulcer
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑁄𑀟 (phoḍa), from Sanskrit स्फोट (sphoṭa). Cognate with Punjabi ਫੋੜਾ (phoṛā) / پھوڑا (phoṛā), Marathi फोड (phoḍ), Konkani फोड (phoḍ), Sinhalese පොළ (poḷa).

ety: caus. of phūṭnā; — phoṛ˚ = Prk. फोड(इ) or फोडे(इ) = S. स्फोटय(ति) caus. of rt. स्फुट्
def: v.t. To break, crack, split, burst open, break open, break to pieces, to shatter; to make manifest, to disclose, divulge, betray (a secret)
subs: v.t. (intensive), To break open, burst open, &c.

def: s.f. An instrument for splitting or cleaving.
s.m. A blister; a swelling, intumescence; anything inflated or puffed out.
s.m. = phūhā or phohā, q.v. s.m. A flock of cotton, &c. ( = phāhā); a fictitious teat or pap (of cotton, &c.), by which milk is given to a young animal when unable to suck the mother.

ety: Prk. पिउहा or पिउच्छा or पिउसिआ = S. पितृ+श्वसा
def: s.f. Father's sister ( = phūpī; phuppī, q.v.; and cf. būʼā).
s.m. A lizard.
adj. & s.f. = phūhaṛ, q.v.
s.m. A woollen rag used to apply grease, ointment, &c. ( = pućārā, q.v.).
v.n. To jump, skip, leap, hop (as birds, frogs, &c.); to skip or dance about (in token of delight).
s.m. = phudkī, q.v. s.f. Jump, skip, hop; — a species of bird, Certhia tula; the purple honey-sucker, a species of humming-bird.
s.f. Jump, skip, hop; — a species of bird, Certhia tula; the purple honey-sucker, a species of humming-bird.
s.m. A flock of cotton, &c. ( = phāhā); a fictitious teat or pap (of cotton, &c.), by which milk is given to a young animal when unable to suck the mother.
s.f. = phuʼār or phuwār, q.v.
adj. &c.; see next.

adj. & s.f. Unlettered, uneducated, undisciplined, rude; stupid, foolish; shameless; obscene; slatternly, slovenly; — a sloven, slattern, a bad housewife; a silly woman; a foolish person, a fool; a shameless woman.
s.f. = phuʼār; phūʼī, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Rude; foolish; obscene; talking obscenely; — one given to obscene talk.

s.f. = phūhaṛāʼī, q.v. s.f. = H پهوهڙپن फूहड़पन phūhaṛpan, s.m. = H پهوهڙپنا फूहड़पना phūhaṛpanā, s.m. Awkwardness, rudeness, slovenliness, untidiness, stupidity, folly, foolishness, silliness; shamelessness; obscenity, scurrility.

s.f. = H پهوهڙپن फूहड़पन phūhaṛpan, s.m. = H پهوهڙپنا फूहड़पना phūhaṛpanā, s.m. Awkwardness, rudeness, slovenliness, untidiness, stupidity, folly, foolishness, silliness; shamelessness; obscenity, scurrility.

s.m. = H پهوهڙائي फूहड़ाई phūhaṛāʼī, s.f. = H پهوهڙپنا फूहड़पना phūhaṛpanā, s.m. Awkwardness, rudeness, slovenliness, untidiness, stupidity, folly, foolishness, silliness; shamelessness; obscenity, scurrility.

s.m. = H پهوهڙائي फूहड़ाई phūhaṛāʼī, s.f. = H پهوهڙپن फूहड़पन phūhaṛpan, s.m. Awkwardness, rudeness, slovenliness, untidiness, stupidity, folly, foolishness, silliness; shamelessness; obscenity, scurrility.
s.f. Small rain, drizzle ( = phuʼār, q.v.); — mildew, mould ( = phaphūṅdī).
s.f. Small rain, drizzle ( = phuʼār, q.v.); — mildew, mould ( = phaphūṅdī).

s.f. = phūṅk, q.v. s.f. The act of blowing; blowing, or blowing up (fire, &c.); — breath, breath of air; blow, puff, whiff, blast
subs: v.n. To be out of breath, the breath to escape, to expire.

def: s.m. A fine sensible young fellow.
s.m. A bladder; — a bag, purse; — a kind of firework, a Roman candle.

v.t. = phūṅknā, q.v. v.n. & s.m. = phuknā, q.v. v.n. To be blown, be inflated; to be blown up (a fire), be blown into a flame; to be set fire to, be burnt; to burn, be consumed (lit. and fig.; — also written phuṅknā)
subs: v.t. To blow into a flame; to set fire to, to burn, consume.

def: v.n. To be blown, be inflated; to be blown up (a fire), be blown into a flame; to be set fire to, be burnt; to burn, be consumed (lit. and fig.; — also written phuṅknā)
subs: v.t. To blow into a flame; to set fire to, to burn, consume.

def: s.f. A hollow piece of bamboo used to blow a fire, a blow-pipe; — a firelock or pistol.
s.m. A flower, a blossom; — a boss, knob, stud, bunch of ribands, rosette, cockade; — the menses (of which children are the blossoms)
subs: a swelling; — a spark of fire; lights or fire

(seen at night); the bones of a corpse after partial cremation; — a ceremony performed (by Musalmāns) in honour of a deceased person on the third day after his death, when a portion of the Qorʼan is recited, and flowers are placed on trays in the midst of the assembly); — a white metal, bell-metal, electrum; — the white marks of leprosy; — a pearl or white opaque spot in the corner of the eye ( = phullī); — a maggot that is hatched in meat or in neglected sores; — a head, stick, small quantity (as we say a head of celery, — e.g. bhang-ke do phūl denā); — the best kind of native wine
v.n. The menses to come on, to have the menses
v.n. The ceremony of phūl (q.v.) to be completed; to complete the forty days of mourning
s.f. A knot of ribands, rosette, cockade
v.n. Sparks to fall (from a burning building, &c.); fire to break out
v.n. To drop flowers (of rhetoric), to speak eloquently; — drops of burning oil to fall (from a lamp); sparks of fire to fall copiously (from a burning mass)
(par), To offer flowers (at a shrine, &c.), to strew flowers (on a tomb)
s.m. A kind of Hindū sport or show
adj. (f. -ī) Like a flower; light as a flower; delicate or frail as a flower
v.n. To live on the fragrance of flowers (said of a woman who eats little)
adj. & s.m. Flowering, in flower; — flower-man
s.m. Wreath or garland of flowers; a delicate, fragile, or frail person or thing
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀼𑀮𑁆𑀮 (phulla), from Sanskrit फुल्ल (phulla). Cognates include Assamese ফুল (phul), Bengali ফুল (phul), Bhojpuri फूल (phūl), Gujarati ફૂલ (phūl), Maithili फूल (phūl), Marathi फूल (phūl), Nepali फूल (phūl), Odia ଫୁଲ (phula), Punjabi پھل / ਫੁੱਲ (phulla), and Sindhi ڦل (phulu).

ety: perf. part. of phūlnā; and = Prk. फुल्लिओ; S. स्फुटित+कः, rt. स्फुट्; and फुल्ल+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Swollen, swelled, puffed up or out; — blossomed, flowered, having flowers
subs: v.n. To be unable to contain oneself (from delight), to be overjoyed, to exult.

ety: rt. of phulānā, q.v.+ Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+ त्वं
def: s.f. Swelling, inflation, distension, puffiness; protuberance.

ety: caus. of phūlnā; — phulā˚ = Prk. फुल्लावे(इ) or फुल्लाव(इ) = S. स्फुट+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to swell; to swell out, puff out; to inflate, distend, expand; to fatten; (met.) to puff (with flattery), to make proud; &c. (see phūlnā).
s.f. = phunaṅg, q.v. s.f. Top, summit; peak, point, end, extremity, tip (of a bough, or of the nose, &c.); projection, anything that projects or stands prominently out.
s.m. = phulāʼo; phulāwaṭ; qq.v.
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Coaxing, wheedling; flattery, &c. ( = phuslāwā).
s.f. A small swollen or puffed cake of fruit and pulse (or of pulse alone) fried in ghī or oil; fritters (syn. pakauṛī; phulkī).

ety: rt. of phulānā, q.v.+ Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+ त्वं
def: s.f. Swelling, inflation, distension, puffiness; protuberance.

s.m. = phulel (the proper form).
s.m. Oil impregnated with essence of flowers by steeping them in it; scented oil; essence (as a perfume).
A cudgelling, a beating.

s.f. = phullī, q.v. s.f. A floweret; a small flower-shaped ornament of gold or silver (worn in the nose, or the ear, or attached to another ornament); — a white speck in the eye, albugo (syn. biyāẓu-ʼl-ʻain); the tumbler of the cock of a musket
subs: (see hūn), s.m. The star-pagoda, a gold coin (of Madras, worth three and a half rupees, or seven shillings sterling).

def: s.f. lit. 'Pouring forth flowers'; a kind of firework in imitation of a fountain, a flower-pot.
s.f. See phuljhaṛī (the proper form).
adj. & s.m. Swollen, puffed up, inflated; risen (as bread or pastry), light; — a blister; — a swollen or puffed ćapātī or cake; — soft earth or ground; — an area or arena for wrestlers.
adj. & s.f. Flowered, figured, embroidered (cloth or paper); — flowered cloth; a tissued flower (on cloth or paper, &c.).
v.t. To inflate, swell out, to expand (as a snake's hood); to expand its hood (a snake).

def: part. Blown, open, expanded (as a flower); in full bloom, in blossom; opened wide, dilated (as the eyes); smiling, gay
subs: s.m. lit. 'Flowery garland'; a species of the atidhriti metre
adj. & s.m. Having the eyes dilated, looking pleased or happy; full-eyed; — a large full eye; — a happy-looking person; a kind of antelope
adj. & s.m. Having full or large eyes; having the eyes dilated (with pleasure), smiling, happy; — a happy-looking person, &c.
phulla-vat, phulla-vant, adj. Expanded, blossoming, blowing, flowering
(˚la+ut˚), s.m. A full-blown lotus or flower.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پھل (phl /⁠phal⁠/) (c. 1518), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀮 (phala), from Sanskrit फल (phála), borrowed from Proto-Dravidian *paẓam (“ripe fruit”).

def: s.m. Anything of the shape of a flower; a speck in the eye, albugo; cataract; — anything swolien; roasted or parched grain
subs: v.n. To remove a cataract, to couch.

ety: dim. of phullā, or of phūl
def: s.f. A floweret; a small flower-shaped ornament of gold or silver (worn in the nose, or the ear, or attached to another ornament); — a white speck in the eye, albugo (syn. biyāẓu-ʼl-ʻain); the tumbler of the cock of a musket
subs: (see hūn), s.m. The star-pagoda, a gold coin (of Madras, worth three and a half rupees, or seven shillings sterling).

def: part. adj. Swollen, blown, inflated, distended.
v.n. = phūlnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit फलति (phalati) or from پھل (phal). Cognate with Punjabi ਫਲਣਾ (phalṇā) / پَھلݨا (phalṇā), Sindhi ڦَلَڻُ (phalaṇu) / फलणु, Gujarati ફળવું (phaḷvũ), Nepali फल्नु (phalnu), Marathi फळणे (phaḷṇe), Bengali ফলা (phola).

ety: phūl˚ = Prk. फुल्ल(इ) = S. स्फुट(ति), rt. स्फुट; also फुल्ल(ति), rt. फुल्ल्, adopted fr. the Prk.
def: v.n. To blossom, blow, flower; to bloom, flourish, to be in health and spirits; — to swell, be inflated, be puffed out; to swell out, to expand (with joy), to be pleased; to be puffed up (with pride, &c.); to be big with child
subs: v.n. To expand with delight, be pleased or glad

def: adj. Having interstices, breaks, chinks, &c.; uncompact; incoherent.
s.f. (Old H.) = phul-wāṛī, q.v. s.f. A flower-garden; an orchard; — offspring, children, family.

s.f. Small rain, &c. = phuʼār, q.v.

s.f. The act of blowing; blowing, or blowing up (fire, &c.); — breath, breath of air; blow, puff, whiff, blast

def: s.f. Hissing, hiss ( = phuṅkār)
v.n. To hiss (as a snake).
v.n. & s.m. = phuknā, q.v. v.n. To be blown, be inflated; to be blown up (a fire), be blown into a flame; to be set fire to, be burnt; to burn, be consumed (lit. and fig.; — also written phuṅknā)

ety: phūṅk˚ = Prk. फुक्क(इ) or फुक्के(इ), fr. S. फूत्+कृ;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To blow, blow on (with the breath); to puff; to blow (a wind instrument); to blow up (fire, &c.); to set on fire, inflame, kindle; to blow or breathe a charm or incantation
subs: v.t. (intensive), To blow away; to waste, squander, run through (wealth, &c.); to blow up (fire), to set fire to, to kindle, inflame; to grieve, afflict, distress, torment; — to puff up, to flatter; to breathe (into one's ear), to influence (one against another); to spread abroad (a report, &c.)
v.n. To tread or walk carefully; to act or proceed carefully or cautiously.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀤𑀺 (phukkadi), from Sanskrit *फूत्कति (*phūtkati), extended from the root फूत् (phūt) or perhaps a back-formation from फूत्कार (phūtkāra, “blowing”). Cognate with Nepali फुक्नु (phuknu), Gujarati ફૂંકવું (phū̃kvũ), Marathi फुंकणे (phuṅkṇe).

ety: doub. caus. of phūṅknā, q.v.; — phuṅkwa˚ = phūṅk+āw redupl. = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आ caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to blow; to cause to be blown; to have (a fire, &c.) blown into flame; to have (a thing) set ablaze, or set fire to.
s.m. A tassel, tuft, bunch (of silk, &c.), the silk lash (of a whip).
s.f. A tassel; a knot; — penis puerilis ( = phunno, q.v.). (see phunaṅg), s.m. Penis puerilis ( = phunnī; syn. nūnī).

r: phunno, phunaṅg, Penis puerilis, phunnī, nūnī
def: (see phunaṅg), s.m. Penis puerilis ( = phunnī; syn. nūnī).
s.m. (prov.) = pūs, q.v. s.m. The ninth solar month of the Hindūs (Dec.-Jan., the full moon of which is near pushya, three stars in Cancer).

def: v.n. To be broken, to be broken into; to be broken down; to be dispersed, be separated, be detached; to separate; to be unpaired; to break, crack, split, burst; to break out or forth; to sprout, shoot, bud, germinate; to burst out or forth, to gush out, to escape (from, -se); to burst out crying; to come into view, to appear, to rise, be spread, be diffused (as a smell, &c.); to be made public, to transpire, to leak out, get wind; to boil (as water); to flourish, get preferment or promotion
subs: v.n. (intensive), To break to pieces, to burst asunder, &c.
v.n. To be broken; to be dispersed; to be unpaired.

pīćheṅ, adv. & postpn. In the rear (of, -ke); in pursuit (of); behind, astern; after (both in time and space); afterwards, subsequently; back, ago; besides; — in the absence (of); after the death (of); — in consequence (of); on, upon; on account (of); for the sake (of), for

pīćhe lagnā or lag-jānā or lag-rahnā

s.f. A tomtit ( = pidṛī; cf. also piddī).
v.n. = pighalnā, q.v. v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse ( = ṭighlānā), to smelt or cast (metal); to soften, assuage, mollify (an angry or a hard person).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi पघिलना (paghilanā), from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀧𑀖𑀺𑀮𑀤𑀺 (*paghiladi), from Sanskrit *प्रघिरति (*praghirati), from प्र- (pra-, “forward, forth”) + घृ (ghṛ, “to moisten”). Cognate with Punjabi پِگَھلݨا (pighalṇā) / ਪਿਘਲਣਾ (pighlaṇā), Marathi पाघळणे (pāghaḷṇe).

def: v.t. = pighlānā, q.v. v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse ( = ṭighlānā), to smelt or cast (metal); to soften, assuage, mollify (an angry or a hard person).
v.n. To melt,to be melted, to dissolve; to be in fusion, to fuse, to flow (as a metal); to melt, be softened, moved, or touched; to yield, give way.
v.t. To cause to be melted, &c.; to melt, &c. = pighlānā.

ety: caus. of pighalnā; pighlā˚ = S. प्रगालय˚
def: v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse ( = ṭighlānā), to smelt or cast (metal); to soften, assuage, mollify (an angry or a hard person).
s.m. Melting, fusion, flux, liquefaction ( = piglāṭ).
v.t. = pighlānā, q.v. v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse ( = ṭighlānā), to smelt or cast (metal); to soften, assuage, mollify (an angry or a hard person).
s.f. Melting, fusion, liquefaction ( = pighlāʼo).
s.f. Fat, grease, tallow.
s.m. A plug for closing the mouth of a granary.
s.m. 'Father's home'; wife's father's house or family (syn. maʼikā).
s.m. A flea ( = pissū).
s.m. A kind of bird (prob. a cuckoo; cf. pik).
s.m. The Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus
subs: (pika + aksha), s.m. 'Cuckoo's eye'; a vegetable and perfume (com. called roćanī)
(˚ka+an˚), s.m. A small bird (com. called ćāṭakīya)
s.m. 'The cuckoo's friend'; the mango tree
pik-bayanī = pik-vayanī, adj. 'Having a voice like the cuckoo,' an epithet of women; — s.f. A cuckoo-voiced woman.

ety: Prk. पिक्का, or पिक्कं; S. पक्का, or पक्कं; cf. also pūg
def: s.f. The juice of the betel leaf chewed and spit out
subs: s.m. pīk-dānī, s.f. A vessel for holding the pīk; a spittoon (syn. ugāl-dān).

def: s.m. The Indian cuckoo ( = pik, q.v.). s.m. The Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus
subs: (pika + aksha), s.m. 'Cuckoo's eye'; a vegetable and perfume (com. called roćanī)
(˚ka+an˚), s.m. A small bird (com. called ćāṭakīya)
s.m. 'The cuckoo's friend'; the mango tree
pik-bayanī = pik-vayanī, adj. 'Having a voice like the cuckoo,' an epithet of women; — s.f. A cuckoo-voiced woman.

def: s.f. = phīk, q.v. s.f. The lash of a whip; the point of a scourge.
